behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is request-builder -> API-client handoff (cross-module wiring/serialization), not OS/network/model quality. Unit was rejected because behavior text says "request sent to the external API," which requires verifying the assembled outbound payload at module boundary. System/Agentic were rejected because no real network or model judgment is needed to detect omission of labels.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the classifier module to a stubbed in-process API client, invoke classify(doc, taxonomy), and assert the captured outbound request payload contains the full taxonomy list exactly once in the expected field.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Real failure often appears at parser/adapter -> normalization handoff (unexpected provider value shape). Unit was rejected because behavior is explicitly about API-returned values entering the module boundary. System/Agentic were rejected: a deterministic stubbed provider response is sufficient; no real network or model-quality dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Use an in-process stub provider to return an out-of-taxonomy label (e.g., "medical") and assert classifier output is exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: This is pure determinism of normalization logic for identical inputs; no module interaction or I/O is required. Higher levels were rejected because behavior text scopes to "normalization step" with identical raw responses.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalize(raw_response, taxonomy) multiple times with the same fixture input and assert identical output label each time.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on repeated live AI API outcomes (non-determinism/consistency of model responses). Integration with stubs would hide real variance. System was considered but rejected because the critical failure boundary is model output consistency, not merely HTTP transport.
behaviors.test_description: Run N repeated real-provider classifications for the same document+taxonomy under fixed request settings; record provider raw outputs and final labels; assert final label changes only when raw provider response changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is config/tokenizer/request-construction interaction before dispatch. Unit was rejected because policy application commonly depends on multiple local modules (config + budgeting + builder). System/Agentic were rejected since enforcement is pre-dispatch and can be validated without real network/model output.
behaviors.test_description: With real local tokenizer/budgeting modules wired and a stub API client, pass an over-budget document and assert the module applies configured truncate/reject policy before any outbound dispatch.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This requires a real AI provider call and real provider token accounting; stubs can mask tokenizer/provider mismatch. Integration was rejected because behavior text is about "provider-reported" usage, which disqualifies fake-only validation. Workflow rejected: single agentic step, not multi-step pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real classification calls for representative documents and assert provider-reported usage fields per call are <= configured budget; fail on any over-budget observation.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Core failure boundary is retry orchestration across client, backoff policy, and caller result propagation. Unit was rejected because this is state/time handoff across modules. System rejected because real network is not required to reproduce 429 + retry path; in-process stub plus controllable clock can expose failures. Agentic rejected because correctness does not depend on model output content.
behaviors.test_description: Use a stub provider that returns rate-limit error then success; run classifier with fake/controlled clock and assert it waits per policy, retries, and returns successful label instead of bubbling error.
